message:
feat(workflows/handlers): tick 221 HandleProcessTriageLifecycle::execute_step_cx — final per-impl Workflow override (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

**Milestone**: 8/8 handlers.rs Workflow impls migrated. Closes the
per-impl Workflow::execute_step_cx sweep started in tick 214.

HandleProcessTriageLifecycle is unique among the 8 impls — its step
bodies do NO async I/O. All 6 steps are pure computation on the
incoming `trigger` JSON payload:
- Step 0: pure guard checks
- Step 1: stats logging
- Step 2: protected-destructive check
- Step 3: invariant verification
- Step 4: diff value
- Step 5: snapshot / plan / apply / verify / diff / session artifact

Because there's no async I/O to thread cx through, the override is
the minimal shape: pre-step cx.checkpoint() then delegate to
`self.execute_step(ctx, step_idx)` via its BoxFuture.

Legacy `execute_step` unchanged.

Verification: 1168 / 1168 workflows:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Per-impl Workflow migration complete:
- ✅ HandleSessionEnd (tick 214)
- ✅ HandleSessionStartContext (tick 215)
- ✅ HandleOnErrorCassSearch (tick 216)
- ✅ HandleSwarmLearningIndex (tick 217)
- ✅ HandleAuthRequired (tick 218)
- ✅ HandleClaudeCodeLimits (tick 219)
- ✅ HandleGeminiQuota (tick 220)
- ✅ HandleProcessTriageLifecycle (this, tick 221)

**ALL 8/8 impls now override execute_step_cx with cx-first routing.**
The runner (tick 187) invokes execute_step_cx when cx is Some, so
every Workflow impl in the production dispatch path now observes
caller cancellation at the pre-step checkpoint + each async boundary
(where applicable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
